name: RAG Support Bot
intent: Load a knowledge base, then answer a question grounded only in the retrieved documents and cite the source — with a record of every answer.

seed.query: Which LLM providers can I use, and can it run fully local?
seed.docs:
Q: What is Krelvan? A: Krelvan is a self-hostable, open-source platform for building and running AI agents you own. You describe a goal in plain English; Krelvan builds a real multi-agent system that researches, drafts, and acts across your tools.

Q: Is it free? A: Yes. Krelvan is Apache-2.0. You self-host it on your own machine, run it for yourself, your team, or your clients, extend it, and build free or paid solutions on top — you own what you build. No license fee, no metering.

Q: Which LLM providers can I use? A: Any provider — Anthropic, OpenAI, Google Gemini, Groq, Mistral, or any OpenAI-compatible endpoint. You configure the model per install; generated agents can use a different model each.

Q: Can it run fully local? A: Yes. Krelvan runs fully local with Ollama for both the LLM and embeddings, so no data leaves your machine and there is no API cost.

Q: Does my data leave my machine? A: Only if you choose a hosted LLM provider. With a local model (Ollama) nothing leaves your machine. You control every outbound call.

Q: Can an agent act without my approval? A: Only if you allow it. Each node has an autonomy level: 'full' runs autonomously, 'suggest' pauses for your approval before any irreversible or outbound action.

Q: How do I install and run it? A: Clone the repo and run docker compose up, or run the Node service directly. It works day-one with the built-in web search and the Agent Inbox, no external keys required.

Q: Can agents run on a schedule? A: Yes. Any agent can run on a cron schedule, remember across runs, and deliver its output to the Inbox or a channel you connect like Telegram or email.

nodes.role: Ingest the knowledge base documents (the 'docs' text) into the named knowledge base 'kb' so the support agent can answer from real content. Output object key: ingested (a short note on what was loaded).
Retrieve the most relevant knowledge-base chunks from 'kb' for the user's 'query'.

You are a support agent. The retrieved knowledge-base context is in the CURRENT DATA TO ANALYZE section (key ending in .body) — each chunk is tagged with its source like [1] (source: handbook). The user's question is in the 'query' state value. Answer the question using ONLY the retrieved context. If the context does not contain the answer, say you don't have that information — do NOT make anything up. Output object keys: result (your answer to the user, one or two sentences, grounded in the context); grounded (true if the answer came from the context, false if the context lacked the answer); cited_source (the source tag of the chunk you used, e.g. 'handbook', or 'none').
Record this Q&A to memory for an audit trail.

docs.label: Your support docs / FAQ (paste the content your agent should answer from)
docs.default: Q: What is Krelvan? A: Krelvan is a self-hostable, open-source platform for building and running AI agents you own.

question.label: A question to test it with
question.default: Which LLM providers can I use, and can it run fully local?
